feedingProfile.preferredNpk: balanced organic-based liquid feed (e.g. 5-10-5 or balanced all-purpose) at half strength for indoor containers; AVOID potassium-rich/tomato feed, which encourages flowering and reduces leaf flavour

commonIssues: Tender annual — killed by frost; foliage blackens and growth stunts at cold temperatures around 10C (50F). Do not plant out until nights stay above 10C.
Hates wet roots overnight: water at the base in the morning, keep foliage dry, and never let it sit waterlogged (overwatering / poor drainage causes root rot and damping off).
Basil downy mildew — fluffy grey-purple growth on leaf undersides; favoured by wet leaves and humidity. Water at soil level and space plants for airflow.
Fusarium wilt and grey mould (Botrytis) in damp, crowded conditions.
Aphids, leafhoppers, and slugs/snails on soft new growth.
Flowering reduces leaf flavour — pinch/harvest tips regularly and remove flower buds to keep plants leafy.

Container plants dry out and run short of nutrients faster than ground-grown plants; a small (~3 L) pot needs frequent watering, so a 6-10 L (20 cm / 8 in wide and deep) pot is easier to keep evenly moist.

source.citation: UMN Extension - Growing basil in home gardens. Needs at least 6-8 hours of bright light/day; water deeply every 7-10 days in beds, containers dry out faster and need more frequent watering; tender annual sown after danger of spring frost passes; cold soil/air stunts growth and can blacken leaves around 50F (10C), really leafs out once temps reach 80F; space 6-12 in apart; 5-10-5 fertiliser once or twice per season (indoor containers: liquid feed at half strength every 4-6 weeks; outdoor containers every 3-4 weeks); seeds germinate in 5-7 days; harvest by pinching just above a leaf pair to encourage branching; basil downy mildew is the main problem.

RHS - How to grow basil. Seeds germinate well at about 20C (68F); only plant out in early summer once night temperatures stay above 10C; final container at least 20cm (8in) wide and deep; water to keep compost evenly moist but avoid splashing leaves and water in the morning as basil hates having wet roots overnight; for container plants apply a balanced organic-based liquid feed and AVOID potassium-rich/tomato feed (encourages flowering); warm, sunny, sheltered position; problems include aphids, damping off, grey mould, powdery/downy mildews, leafhoppers and slugs/snails.

Clemson HGIC - Herbs. Almost all herbs (incl. basil) grow best in a site sunny at least 6 hours/day; water thoroughly then allow soil to dry out somewhat before watering again; basil is a tender annual sown after all danger of frost is past and is killed by frost; most herbs do not need highly fertile soil (excess fertility reduces flavour); harvest leaves frequently to prevent flowering, which reduces flavour; aphids common in crowded, succulent growth.

Confidence note - several values are conservative interpretations rather than directly stated numbers. wateringProfile.baseIntervalDays (1.5, carried from the v1 seed) reflects a small container that dries fast and basil's low drying tolerance; UMN's '7-10 days' applies to in-ground beds, not pots. hotWeatherMultiplier (1.5) and rainSkipThresholdMm (15) are estimates consistent with 'water more often in hot weather'. containerProfile.recommendedMinLiters (3, from seed) is a usable small-pot minimum; idealMin/Max (6-10 L) derive from RHS's 20cm/8in final container. temperatureProfile.heatStressC (35) is conservative - basil is heat-loving and sources cite no firm upper damage threshold (it 'really leafs out' at ~27C/80F), so this is a low-confidence upper bound. lightProfile.targetSunHours (7) is the midpoint of the cited 6-8 hour range.
